content.problemStatement:
To make Yalalovichik even more satisfied and happy, Jafar decided to invent Yalalovichik strings. A string is called a Yalalovichik string if the set of all of its distinct non-empty substrings is equal to the set of all of its distinct non-empty subsequences.

You are given a string SSS. You need to find the number of its distinct non-empty substrings which are Yalalovichik strings.

**Note:** A string AAA is called a subsequence of a string BBB if AAA can be formed by erasing some characters (possibly none) from BBB. A string AAA is called a substring of a string BBB if it can be formed by erasing some characters (possibly none) from the beginning of BBB and some (possibly none) from the end of BBB. Two substrings or subsequences are considered different if they are different strings.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2 * 10^6
S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N = |S|. The second line contains the string S.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of distinct Yalalovichik substrings of S.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The distinct Yalalovichik substrings are "x", "xx" and "xxx".
The distinct Yalalovichik substrings are "a", "aa", "aaa" and "aaaa".
The distinct Yalalovichik substrings are "a", "b", "c", "d" and "e".

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to generate all possible substrings of the given string S and check if each substring is a Yalalovichik string. A string is a Yalalovichik string if the set of all its distinct non-empty substrings is equal to the set of all its distinct non-empty subsequences. We can use a brute force approach to generate all substrings and subsequences, and then compare the two sets. If they are equal, the substring is a Yalalovichik string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^3) — for each test case, we generate all substrings (O(N^2)) and for each substring, we generate all subsequences (O(2^N)) and compare the two sets (O(N^2))
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — we need to store all substrings and subsequences
